title: AI CodeFix | SonarQube for VS Code Documentation
content:
# AI CodeFix in your IDE

## [AI CodeFix in your IDE](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/ai-capabilities/ai-codefix/#ai-codefix "AI CodeFix in your IDE")

AI CodeFix is a feature that generates AI-generated fix suggestions in (SonarQube Server, Cloud) and SonarQube for VS Code. It must be activated by a SonarQube Server or Cloud administrator. See the [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/instance-administration/ai-features/enable-ai-codefix/ "SonarQube Server") or [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/administering-sonarcloud/ai-features/enable-ai-codefix/ "SonarQube Cloud") documentation for more details about enabling AI CodeFix.

### [About AI CodeFix](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/ai-capabilities/ai-codefix/#about-ai-codefix "About AI CodeFix")

Running SonarQube for VS Code in connected mode with SonarQube Server 2025.3+ or SonarQube Cloud is required to get AI-generated fix suggestions in your IDE. See the [Connected mode setup](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode-setup/#sonarqube-cloud "Connected mode setup") page for details.

The suggestions are generated using the LLM selected when enabling the feature on the server. Please see the [SonarQube Server](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/instance-administration/ai-features/enable-ai-codefix/ "SonarQube Server") or [SonarQube Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/administering-sonarcloud/ai-features/enable-ai-codefix/ "SonarQube Cloud") instructions for more details.

### [Getting AI-generated fix suggestions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/ai-capabilities/ai-codefix/#getting-aigenerated-fix-suggestions "Getting AI-generated fix suggestions")

With AI-CodeFix enabled in SonarQube Cloud, issues eligible for an AI-generated fix are identifiable by the **✧˖°** icon found in the SonarQube for VS Code Quick Fix menu. You may need to restart your IDE if the feature was recently enabled in SonarQube Cloud and the server has not yet synced with SonarQube for VS Code.

To generate a fix suggestion:

- In VS Code, select an issue in the PROBLEMS view panel or click the lightbulb in the left margin of the VS Code editor to reveal available Quick Fixes for your issue.
- Select **SonarQube: ✧˖° Fix with AI CodeFix ‘<*****YOUR\_ISSUE\_DESCRIPTION*****\>’**

  - An AI CodeFix snippet, or series of snippets if your issue is an [injection vulnerability](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/taint-vulnerabilities/ "injection vulnerability") or something like a duplicated string that requires multiple snippets, will be generated and displayed with a diff view in the REFACTOR PREVIEW view panel.
- To open a contextual diff view, select the checkbox and click on the snippet in the REFACTOR PREVIEW view panel.
- Select the snippets you would like to keep, and then select **Apply** or **Discard** to accept or decline the AI CodeFix suggestion and close the REFACTOR PREVIEW view panel.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Select your AI-generated fix suggestion (the AI CodeFix feature) in the REFACTOR PREVIEW view panel to see the diff view in the code editor.
 ](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/5140a59a-1f65-415d-879f-85598926a03e/sq-vscode-ai-codefix-open-diff-view.png?w=3074&h=1262&auto=format&fit=crop "Select your AI-generated fix suggestion (the AI CodeFix feature) in the REFACTOR PREVIEW view panel to see the diff view in the code editor.

")

It's also possible to use the **Open in IDE** button from SonarQube Cloud to navigate to your issue in the code editor. See the article on [Opening issues in the IDE](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/investigating-issues/#opening-issues-in-the-ide "Opening issues in the IDE") for more information.

## [Rules covered with AI CodeFix](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/ai-capabilities/ai-codefix/#ai-codefix-rules "Rules covered with AI CodeFix")

Below you'll find a list of Sonar Rules that are eligible for use with our AI CodeFix feature. We've tested each of these rules in the supported AI models and given them a confidence score to ensure they can effectively help you resolve issues. Once a rule passes our certification, it's added to the AI CodeFix service and becomes available to you. We're constantly working to expand this list by evaluating more rules and enhancing our AI capabilities.

Each collapsible holds a list of rules for the listed language. You will find instructions inside on how to find the detailed rule description on the [Sonar Rules website](https://rules.sonarsource.com/ "Sonar Rules website").

AI CodeFix rules for C++

#### [AI CodeFix rules for C++](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/ai-capabilities/ai-codefix/#ai-codefix-rules-for-cpp "AI CodeFix rules for C++")

Go to the [Sonar Rules website](https://rules.sonarsource.com/ "Sonar Rules website") to search for more information about your rule.

- select any rule at [https://rules.sonarsource.com/cpp/](https://rules.sonarsource.com/cpp/ "https://rules.sonarsource.com/cpp/")
- replace the RSPEC number in the rule's URL with the relevant rule number listed below.

For example, to read about rule `cpp:S1048`, go to [https://rules.sonarsource.com/cpp/RSPEC-1048/](https://rules.sonarsource.com/cpp/RSPEC-1048/ "https://rules.sonarsource.com/cpp/RSPEC-1048/")
